name: Server vs Client Components Decision
description: Default to Server Components. Add 'use client' only when you need interactivity, browser APIs, or React hooks. Interactivity should live at the leaf level, not the root.

name: Server Actions in App Router
description: Use Server Actions for all form submissions and mutations. Define in separate server-only modules. Validate inputs server-side. Use revalidatePath/revalidateTag to refresh affected cache.

name: Data Fetching and Cache Control
description: Use fetch() with Next.js cache options in Server Components. Understand the difference between static, dynamic, and revalidating data.

name: Streaming with Suspense
description: Wrap slow data-fetching components in Suspense to stream HTML progressively. Fast parts of the page reach the user before slow ones finish.

name: Metadata API for SEO
description: Use Next.js 13+ Metadata API to set page-level and template-level meta tags, Open Graph images, and robots directives from Server Components.

name: Middleware for Auth and Redirects
description: Use Next.js middleware (middleware.ts at project root) for auth guards, locale detection, A/B testing, and geo-based redirects — before the request hits a route.

name: Image Optimization with next/image
description: Always use next/image instead of . It provides automatic format conversion (WebP/AVIF), lazy loading, size optimization, and prevents layout shift via aspect ratio.

name: Route Handlers (API Routes)
description: Use Route Handlers (app/api/.../route.ts) for webhooks, third-party callbacks, binary responses, or when you need full HTTP control. Prefer Server Actions for mutations from your own UI.

name: Parallel and Intercepting Routes
description: Use parallel routes (@folder) for simultaneous layout slots and intercepting routes ((...)) for modal patterns that show content without navigating away from the current page.
template:
## Parallel & Intercepting Routes ### Parallel routes — dashboard with independent slots ``` app/dashboard/ ├── layout.tsx # receives @analytics and @team as props ├── @analytics/ │ └── page.tsx └── @team/ └── page.tsx ``` ```tsx // app/dashboard/layout.tsx export default function Layout({ children, analytics, team }) { return (
{analytics} {team}
); } ``` ### Intercepting routes — photo modal ``` app/ ├── photos/ │ ├── [id]/ │ │ └── page.tsx # full page when navigated directly │ └── page.tsx └── @modal/ └── (.)photos/ └── [id]/ └── page.tsx # intercepted — shows as modal from /photos ``` **When to use:** - Modals that are deep-linkable (share the URL) → intercepting routes - Dashboard segments that load and refresh independently → parallel routes - Soft navigation shows modal; hard refresh shows full page → both together

name: Font Optimization with next/font
description: Use next/font/google or next/font/local to self-host fonts with automatic subsetting, zero layout shift, and no external network requests at runtime.
